title: EC weekly meeting 4.13.26
summary:
### Financial Performance - Week & Period Overview

- Week sales: $14.6M (beat LY by $1.4M, on plan, missed forecast by $145K)
- Course materials down $700K due to access decline
- Apparel down $1.2M, tech down $700K
- Period to date: -2% comp (would be -8% without tech favorability)
- Apple promotion timing shift created $1.6M favorable comp vs LY

### Course Materials Performance

- Access driving primary decline as telegraphed in recent forecasts
- Student purchase flat to forecast, recovered week 1 losses
- Access visibility increased from $2.6M to $2.7M
- Power BI reporting issues - pending orders may not be refreshing properly
- Three key factors impacting forecast:
  - Store timing shifts
  - One store closure
  - Bypass title timing differences vs LY

### General Merchandise Results

- Soft lines: $8.2M total with significant shifts
- Final Four impact: $800K boost (Florida/Houston championship teams)
- Event shifts: ~$650K, total shifts ~$700K expected back in next 2 weeks
- Period to date: -4% soft lines comp, -11% period to date
- New/closed store impact: ~$200K negative on soft lines

### Hard Lines & Technology

- $6.4M total, up $1.9M vs LY week
- Apple sale performance: -35% vs LY week 4 timing
- $200K in backorders expected to fill
- $700K in guaranteed sales likely won't hit April (expected May)
- Strong ID performance: +47% (or +15% excluding tech)

### Collections Update

- Last week in line with LY, $1M below prior year
- Still up $9M through 2 weeks of P10 vs LY
- Up $92M year over year overall
- Strong percentage metrics maintained
- New AR collection letter going out this week
- 2-week window before summer materials provisioning

### Workforce Management & Service

- 6% over productive hours last week due to workforce reduction timing
- 70+ FT team members reduced from stores
- Hours should balance over next 2 weeks
- OSAT: 81% April MTD vs 82% LY end
- Top/bottom RM performance tracking now shared regularly
- Service improvement focus on policy changes to reduce store escalations

### Marketing & Digital Performance

- Traffic: +6% comp (better measure: GM traffic -3%, +21% excluding basketball comps)
- Email/SMS: +26% YoY, GM up 31%, tech up 167%
- Paid media: 5.7 ROAS, 130% growth in visits
- Apple sale messaging pivot improved performance from -65% to -35% vs LY
- Light loyalty program: $460K revenue YTD vs $235K in 3 months LY

### Technology & Systems Updates

- Loyalty program launch at risk due to Shopify POS integration issues
- Cross-store functionality vs single-store approach decision needed
- Slack rollout expanding: regions 35, 36, 40 as pilots
- Company-wide Slack training scheduled for next week
- BI platform progress: 3-month timeline to July 7 production go-live
- Claude AI enterprise evaluation ongoing for broader rollout

### Next Steps

- Architecture review meeting April 28 for BI platform
- Partner performance survey results to be shared next week
- Service policy recommendations coming to smaller group
- Revenue Vision product demo scheduled for Friday
- Loyalty program stakeholder meeting Thursday with Shopify/P3/Talon One

title: Consumer Marketing weekly report out 4.13.26
summary:
### Weekly Performance Review

- Traffic down 6% due to abnormal single-page visits
  - Rental page visits from purchase decision users
  - My Account page traffic from order status checks
  - Homepage driven by IU performance across social, paid, direct, email
  - Spread across multiple sources, not bot traffic
  - Likely correlation with backorders and auto-cancel orders during Apple sale
- Hot market impact from Houston/Florida performance last year
  - Negative impact on GM, return users across all channels
  - Removing hot market schools improves all metrics

### Apple Sale Performance Analysis

- Traffic gains misleading - driven by comp week shift, not improvement
- Year-over-year comparison (promo to promo):
  - Orders down 44%
  - Promo dollars down 52%
  - Apple dollars down 52%
  - Traffic down ~35%
- Weaker offer this year: "Apple on sale up to 10%" vs last year's "up to 50% off"
  - Limited end-of-life inventory compared to last year
- Team responded quickly to slow start:
  - Monday: down 65% -> switched messaging Tuesday
  - Tuesday: down 45% -> updated hero asset Wednesday
  - Wednesday: down 35%
- Inventory constraints on MacBook Neo models
  - All Neo inventory sold out by Friday morning
  - Auto-cancel issues across models

### Program Updates

- NYU Instagram growth: +3,371 followers since August 2024 (215% relative change)
- SMD recruitment: 7 of 12 positions filled by end of week
  - Stanford and Northeastern have transitioning SMDs
  - HR supporting remaining 5 open positions
- Loyalty program setbacks from Wheaton College UAT testing:
  1. Store associate interface slow, poor search querying
  2. Shopify recommends single-university build vs multi-university
  3. Cannot use loyalty redemption with discounts/promotions
  4. Overall Shopify POS interface issues
- Dependencies: PIM launch delayed to 4/28 for two stores, affecting promotions rollout

title: GM weekly meeting 4.13.26
summary:
### GM Performance Overview

- Overall GM up 23% at comp stores last week
- Strong growth driven by Apple sale
- Apparel decline primarily from men's basketball championship impact
  - Florida down 85%
  - Houston down ~24k
  - Wake Forest and Northeastern ~100%
- Course materials down 4%

### Traffic & Sales Analysis

- GM traffic decline driven by Florida and Houston
  - Excluding those markets: 20%+ growth
- Top 75 stores significantly impacted by men's basketball comparison
  - Excluding Florida/Houston: traffic up 40%, sales up 44%
- Non-top 75 stores flat week
  - Western Michigan affected by Frozen Four results
  - Grad sales down, primarily Sienna (likely grad shift)

### Apple Sale Performance

- Drove ~1,000 orders online
- Generated $655k in total basket sales
- Down ~50% vs last year (6-day promotion vs current shorter run)
- Key schools affected: ASU, Florida, NYU, other tech schools

### Digital Marketing Results

- Comp traffic up 6% (12 consecutive weeks of growth)
  - Driven by abnormal single-page visits to home/account/rental pages
  - Didn't convert to GM and course material traffic
- New users strong: +43k visits (13 consecutive weeks growth)
- Return users down 4% (-10k visits, driven by Florida/Houston)
- Email performance up 17% (+14k visits)
- Paid advertising: $84k spend, 5.7 ROAS, visits up 130% year-over-year

### Social & Content Performance

- 242 content pieces delivered
- 1,000+ new followers/fans
- 2.43M organic impressions
- NYU Instagram surpassed 8,000 followers (biggest account in program)
- Strong athletic partnerships: Purdue, Georgia, IU collaborations

### Week Ahead Focus

- Continue pushing 75% off sale and clearance inventory
- Launch digital teaser campaigns for grad appreciation event (Thursday 4/23)
- Grad appreciation events promotion starting this week

title: Shopify 4.14.26
summary:
### Shopify Rollout Status & Timeline

- 73 physical stores with e-commerce live, 8-9 stores with POS live
- Entering "pause and enhance" period - Jackie and Trisha ahead of schedule on business readiness
- Talent One PIM integration campaigns being set up for 4/28 timeline
- UI and catalog standardization work with P3 team for content management system
- Next week: Type 17 launch at University of Cincinnati (5/17)
  - Launching Shopify and Pipe 17 simultaneously
  - Store fulfillment in Shopify, cross-store activities through Pipe 17
- Target: 4/28 for all systems live, then launch 50 stores at once
- 4/28-5/5: Catch-up period for previously delayed stores
- 5/12: "Brave new world" - new stores with full PIM, Talent One, Pipe 17, digital marketing, new UI

### PIM Integration Progress

- Integration technically ready, waiting on catalog loading and workflow acceptance
- Over 250 vendors complete on merch ops side
- Trisha's team adding assets and imagery
- Focusing on largest vendors first for initial store coverage
- Historical data being pushed through system for proper PIM downloads
- 4/28 PIM go-live confirmed for first set of stores

### POS Scanning Solution & Store Experience

- Enhanced scan recovery app with 0.6 seconds per scan (tested)
- Loaded UPC combinations with leading zeros into Shopify as meta objects
- Serial scanning capability - no need to click add button
- Testing at Wheaton College Thursday with Melech and Samya
- Shopify team spent weekend mocking up new workflows after store visits
- Weekly Thursday calls established with P3 team and Shopify participation
- Field associates (Sarah from TCU, John from Lean College) providing regular feedback
- Shopify collecting data to influence product roadmap for enterprise clients
  - Focus on SKU management and automated gift card management
- 5/12 target date to evaluate if POS rollout can accelerate or needs to decelerate

### Traffic & SEO Performance Analysis

- Week 41 results: In-store sales flat YoY, online down 15% for Shopify stores
  - Marion store driving majority of decline due to term shift (17k online impact)
- First week with organic search visits up YoY despite lower revenue
- Scale group stores (most recent launches) showing -26% traffic decline
- Organic search visits up 9% overall at total group of stores
- SEO improvements: Dynamic metadata, canonical URLs being implemented
- Click-through rates and ranking positions improving
- Expected 3-6 month recovery period for Google recrawling after URL changes

### AI Shopping Assistant & Digital Enhancements

- AI shopping assistant live on College Nation
  - Handles apparel gift recommendations, cart additions, order viewing
  - Built with P3 team, no external apps
  - Will roll out across all Shopify-aligned stores after feedback iteration
- Digital Wave AI agent for vendor spec import
  - Reads PDF vendor files, extracts all data automatically
  - Creates 50-60 SKUs from single PDF document
  - Eliminates manual keying and copy-pasting for buyers
  - Early May launch planned with enhancements

### Outstanding Issues & Next Steps

- Financial reconciliation challenges scaling with store count
- Asset protection risks: Chargeback acceptance rates, line item audit trail
- Roles-based access beta release expected this week from Shopify
- Weekly P1 defects being addressed (refunds, shipping charges, ERP integration)
- Need comprehensive pre/post launch analytics deep dive
  - Critical KPIs, operational metrics, fulfillment rates
  - Week-by-week impact analysis for launched stores
  - Present to steering committee or decisions committee next Friday

title: Advisory Digital Agency 1:1 4.15.26
summary:
### New Customer Growth & Digital Strategy Performance

- New customer acquisition showing strong momentum with 13% year-over-year growth
  - Digitally sourced new customers up 54% - significant acceleration
  - Growth driven by strategic exclusions in Meta ad units to eliminate retargeting existing customers
  - Budget reallocation toward top-of-funnel prospecting for emblematic GM items
  - Moving away from customers searching for course materials or tech products
- Marketing channel distribution and evolution
  - Q3 spend allocation: 50% Google, 48% Meta, 2% TikTok (expanding channel testing)
  - Continued strategic pullback from tech and course materials categories
  - Focus intensifying on higher margin emblematic merchandise
- Attribution and reporting challenges identified
  - Need better storytelling around marketing-sourced ecommerce customers over time
  - Current reporting doesn't clearly demonstrate incremental spend value to executive team
  - Opportunity to show dramatic efficiency improvements from funnel rebalancing
  - Anniversary date of Advisory engagement (~April 1st) creates clean comparison point

### AI Testing Results & Platform Innovations

- Google AI Search feature comprehensive split testing
  - Bookstore campaign delivered +16% efficiency improvement
  - Non-brand apparel campaign showed -4.7% performance decline
  - AI functionality: keyword injection and copy optimization
  - Captures user intent from misspellings, abbreviations, shorthand searches
  - Feature replacing Dynamic Search Ads completely in July 2024
  - Plan to retest Q3/Q4 2024 for performance improvements
- School targeting strategy: National vs State geographic testing
  - Inconsistent results requiring school-by-school analysis approach
  - Strong national performance: Michigan State, Stanford (large out-of-state populations)
  - Better state targeting: Baylor, Wake Forest (more regional alumni networks)

### Shopify Migration Strategic Opportunities

- Campus creator commission program development
  - Leverage high-quality student content creators not employed by campus bookstores
  - Commission-based model for linked sales of campus merchandise
  - Platform evaluation: Growy Social vs Social Snowball
  - Test at TCU (only major Shopify-migrated school currently)
- Offline attribution expansion for in-store conversion tracking
  - Two options: eLLAR vs Blot Out
  - Estimated cost: ~$400/month per location
  - Enables store-visit-focused ad campaigns with measurable ROI

### Budget Management & Performance Analytics

- Q3 comprehensive performance metrics
  - Total investment: $1.6M driving $12M revenue (7.4 ROAS)
  - New customer generation: 11K customers at $176 lifetime value
  - Total LTV impact: $2M from new customer acquisition
  - New customer growth: 54-55% quarter-over-quarter increase
- Attribution reporting methodology improvements required
  - Current 1.3 net margin ROI conservative (Adobe Analytics data only)
  - Need clean separation of trackable vs non-trackable channel performance

### Innovation Pipeline

- Shopify Shop app advertising pilot (pay-per-purchase model)
- Industry trends quarterly briefing for executive team
- Creator program financial modeling with finance team
- Course materials business impact deep-dive analysis

title: Shopify migration and marketing strategy 4.17.26
summary:
### ESP Migration Strategy

- Oracle Responsys being sunset, need new ESP by January
- RFP process required - marketing must drive selection, not IT
- Hajar moving fast on Bloom Reach recommendation for search/recommendations
- Risk of repeating Shopify mistakes where insufficient discovery led to problems
- Focus on specific use cases rather than all-in-one solutions

### Site Design Issues

- No clear north star for final site design vision
- Lobo Den site created by Hajar showing poor performance data
- Daniel Green's UX team sidelined during process
- ASU.2 closer to right direction but not complete vision
- Need design system strategy vs. current ad-hoc approach

### Marketing Performance

- Google: 8.8 ROAS, $800K spend, $7.1M attribution, 2.3 ROI at net margin
- Paid Social: 6.0 ROAS, $798K spend, $4.8M attribution
- Target 3.5 average ROAS across channels for profitability
- Email capture improved from ~17% to approaching 40%
- Goal: double e-commerce sales over 3 years
- Need waterfall analysis of growth drivers

### Operational Readiness

- Concerns about post-launch site management scalability
- Trisha creating requirements list for operational handover
- Current requirements mostly in individual heads, not systematized

### Next Steps

- Review operational readiness with Trisha
- Connect with Andrew Solomon on LinkedIn/Google advertising
- Explore extended landing pages SEO strategy

title: Social media strategy expansion 4.17.26
summary:
### Budget Overview

- Monthly incremental budget: $127K across all proposed initiatives
- All tactics are low-risk with flexibility to adjust/stop based on performance

### Content Creation Network (Shopify Integration)

- Test with $10K/month using Growy or Social Snowball
- 10% commission structure targeting $100K return
- Requires Jennifer Spray approval for commission-based operations
- Scaling: proof of concept at 2 locations, then contract support, then junior headcount
- Limited to current live Shopify sites only

### Platform Expansion Plans

- Meta store expansion: Add 50 stores at $500/month average (good/better/best tiers)

- YouTube expansion:
  - SMD content as shorts (minimal amplify budget ~$2-4K)
  - Pre-roll/midstream ads with surgical targeting
  - Collaboration opportunities with established creators

### SMD Program Enhancement

- Current: Top 40 schools approved in budget discussions with Brian Neal
- Proposed expansion to top 75 based on soft lines GM sales
- 10-hour minimum for additional schools (similar to Starbucks model)
- Top performing content: athletic collaborations, Lululemon/Free People, giveaways

### Next Steps

- Include incremental costs and expected ROI
- Prioritize YouTube for summer implementation
- Demo Social Snowball for cost clarity

title: AR Contact List Data Quality and Email Campaign Cleanup with Lori 4.20.26
summary:
### Data Quality Crisis

- AR contact database completely unreliable since Nancy Ball's departure (~1 year ago)
  - 54 emails sent to Miami-Dade alone
  - 45 contacts missing actual names, just email addresses
  - Hundreds of bounce-backs from people no longer at organizations
- Marketing team flagged data concerns but severity wasn't communicated clearly
  - Katie repeatedly asked "are you sure?" about sending emails
  - Team knew AR data was "a mess" but didn't convey full scope
- CFO list (200 contacts) is clean and recently purchased

### System Ownership and Responsibilities

- Marketing team maintains Salesforce for buying decision contacts only
  - Purchases and updates contacts involved in RFPs, daily interactions
  - Sales and marketing contact roles covered
- Finance contacts never maintained in Salesforce historically
  - AR data lives outside marketing's control systems
  - Provided as spreadsheets from finance team

### Immediate Damage Control

- Laurie handling 300+ responses from email blast
  - Compiling bounce-back list for removal
  - Most recipients understanding after explanation
- HubSpot can automatically purge bounced emails
- Problematic AR list permanently retired

### Long-term Solution

- New AR system (Majia) will house all AR contacts
  - Tracks all customer invoice communications
  - Can share data bidirectionally with Salesforce
- Proposed verification: semi-annual contact confirmation emails to CFOs
- Decision needed: Salesforce as complete customer record vs. buying contacts only

### Next Steps

- Leann's team to provide CFO list to Laurie
- Investigate Miami-Dade contact source
- Field teams to provide current AR contact lists for verification

title: FY27 rental strategy 4.21.26
summary:
### Marketing Campaign Updates

- New shelf tag design with rent and save banner for accounting sections
- Sign holder size (122 x 28) insufficient - needs ceiling overhang type signage
- Major execution concerns across 1,000 stores
  - Field compliance currently poor despite CEO emphasis
  - New signage kits and audits launched in recent weeks
  - CM3s spending insufficient time in stores (need 70% store time)
- Shelf tag quality issues observed at TCU and other locations
  - Illegible prints from low ink being installed anyway
  - Need clear policy: don't install unreadable signage

### FY27 Rental Pricing Strategy

- New rental tiers with 40% off new retail where ebook equivalent exists
  - Strategy shift from competing with new retail to competing with ebooks
  - Currently piloted at 6 core schools, expanding to 130 summer locations
  - Pricing based on ebook being 40% less than new retail on average
- Where no ebook equivalent exists: 25% off new retail (breakeven with cost)
  - Competes with used retail pricing
  - Factors in declining buyback opportunities
- Used rental pricing structure:
  1. Year supply vs demand available: 80% off new retail immediately
  2. Limited supply: Start at 50% off new retail
- Exclusions apply mainly to new books (access codes, workbooks)
- Publisher rental pricing controlled by publishers (e.g., Pearson $10M program)

### Execution Challenges & Improvements Needed

- Student confusion about rental eligibility
  - Books not clearly marked as rental-eligible on shelf
  - POS prompts associates but unclear to customers
  - Need better signage and associate training
- Shelf tag redesign priority for fall implementation
  - Current tags too cluttered and confusing
  - Digital format pricing unclear (perpetual vs 180-day access)
  - Sticker placement issues pointing to wrong information
- Historical benchmark request: rental coverage vs sales data from previous high-coverage periods
  - Need data on 40-80% title availability impact on rental volume
  - Required for conservative budgeting approach
